title: PresentationLayer
description: PRESENTATION STORE — the patient record data the AI Doctor is permitted to see. Structured around the Digital Tablet (digital_tablet_omnibus.json) FHIR R4 schema but expressed in natural patient language and deliberately incomplete relative to ground truth. Three principles govern what appears here: (1) NO diagnostic spoilers — no SNOMED diagnosis codes, no confirmed diagnosis labels, no ClinicalImpression assessment, no risk stratification scores; (2) PATIENT VOICE — all symptom descriptions are in the patient's own words, not clinical terminology; (3) SELECTIVE DISCLOSURE — what the patient volunteers versus what must be elicited is controlled entirely by the companion conversational_policy file, not by what appears here. The gap between this file and the ground_truth_node is what makes the evaluation non-trivial.

case_id.description: Back-reference to the parent case envelope. Must match CaseEnvelope.case_id exactly.

demographics.description: Patient demographic data. Maps to Digital Tablet Part A — Patient resource. IHI, Medicare number, and MRN are present for identity but redacted from AI Doctor context in production (accessed only via identity-au MCP server receipt). All other demographic fields are visible to the AI Doctor. fhir_resource: Patient. au_core_profile: http://hl7.org.au/fhir/core/StructureDefinition/au-core-patient

age.description: Patient age in years at time of encounter. Maps to Patient.birthDate (derived). Clinically significant — age directly affects differential probability and pharmacology safety (paediatric/geriatric thresholds).

sex_at_birth.description: Biological sex at birth. Maps to Patient.gender (FHIR R4) and SNOMED 248153007|Male| / 248152002|Female|. Clinically relevant for risk stratification (CVD risk models differ by sex), pharmacokinetics, and screening recommendations. Distinct from gender_identity.

gender_identity.description: Optional. Patient's gender identity. Maps to Patient.extension[gender-identity] (AU extension). Only populate when clinically relevant to the case (e.g. gender-affirming care context, hormone therapy). SNOMED 446151000124109|Identifies as male| / 446141000124107|Identifies as female| / 33791000087105|Non-binary|.

pregnancy_status.description: Optional. Maps to Patient.extension or SDOH context. 'unknown_possible' is the realistic default for adult females of reproductive age who have not volunteered this information — triggers pregnancy_check in pharm-check. 'not_applicable' for males, post-menopausal females, and pre-pubertal patients.

occupation.description: Optional. Current occupation. Maps to Patient SDOH Observation (SNOMED 159100006, LOINC 11341-5). Be specific — occupational exposures, shift work, manual labour, and healthcare worker status are diagnostically relevant. 'unemployed', 'retired', and 'student' are valid values.

living_situation.description: Optional. Housing and cohabitation context. Maps to Patient SDOH (SNOMED 365508006, LOINC 71802-3). Affects safety-netting feasibility — 'lives alone, no next of kin nearby' changes the escalation threshold. 'residential care' flags geriatric complexity.
examples: lives with partner and two children

geographic_context.description: Optional. ARIA remoteness classification. Maps to Patient.extension[remoteness-area] (AU extension). Maps to Digital Tablet Patient.extensions.ARIA_remoteness codes RA1-RA5. Critically affects safety-netting — 'go to ED now' has vastly different urgency implications for RA4/RA5 patients (3+ hour travel time) versus RA1. WA context: significant proportion of population is RA3-RA5.

language_and_literacy.description: Optional. Language and health literacy context. Maps to Patient.communication (language system urn:ietf:bcp:47) and Patient.extension[interpreter-required] (AU extension, SNOMED 315244000|Interpreter not required| / 315045002|Interpreter required|).

primary_language.description: Patient's primary language. BCP-47 language code or common name. Maps to Patient.communication.language_code.

english_proficiency.description: English communication proficiency. 'interpreter_required' cases should test whether the AI Doctor flags interpreter need and adapts communication accordingly. Maps to Patient.extension[interpreter-required].

health_literacy_level.description: Estimated health literacy level. Affects the vocabulary and complexity the patient uses in their opening complaint and subsequent responses. 'low' literacy patients describe symptoms in very lay terms; 'high' literacy patients may use some medical terminology.

indigenous_status.description: Optional. Australian indigenous status. Maps to Patient.extension[indigenous-status] (AU extension) and NCTS CodeSystem australian-indigenous-status-1. SNOMED 32570681000036106|Non-Indigenous Australian|. Clinically relevant for targeted screening (rheumatic heart disease, T2DM, CKD higher prevalence), culturally appropriate care, and some Medicare items. Include only when clinically relevant to the case.

contact_nok.description: Optional. Next of kin or emergency contact. Maps to Patient.contact. Relevant to safety-netting (who to call, who brings them to ED) and substitute decision-making in capacity-affected cases.

available.description: Whether NOK is contactable/present. Relevant for paediatric cases (parent available?) and incapacity scenarios.

opening_complaint.description: The ONLY information the AI Doctor receives at the start of the encounter. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags and Composition.section[HPC] (LOINC 10164-2). CRITICAL RULE: this text must be in natural patient language — no clinical terminology, no SNOMED codes, no diagnostic hints. Write it as the patient would actually type it, including colloquialisms, minimisation, catastrophising, or vagueness as appropriate for the communication_profile in this case.

verbatim_patient_text.description: Exactly what the patient types/says first. This is the seed of the entire encounter. The quality of the AI Doctor's history-taking determines how much additional information it can elicit beyond this opening. Maps to Composition.section[HPC] free text, LOINC 10164-2. Must not contain SNOMED codes, ICD codes, or clinical terminology the patient would not actually use.
examples: Hi, I've had this indigestion feeling on and off since yesterday afternoon, kind of a burning in my chest. I tried some antacid but it's not really helping.
My back has been killing me for the past two weeks. It started after I helped my mate move house. Taking ibuprofen but it's not doing much.
I've been feeling really tired all the time for a few months now, even when I sleep a lot. My wife said I should get checked out.
I need a repeat script for my blood pressure tablet. The white one, I forget what it's called.
My 4-year-old has had a fever since yesterday and won't stop crying. Should I be worried?

stated_reason_for_presenting_today.description: Optional. Why today specifically — often diagnostically and behaviourally significant. Distinct from the chief complaint — this is the trigger for seeking care now. Maps to Encounter.reasonCode or Condition._freetext_HPC_tags context. 'My partner made me call', 'I can't sleep', 'It's getting worse' are examples that reveal health anxiety, social dynamics, and symptom trajectory.
examples: Can't make it to school tomorrow if this doesn't settle
It's been getting worse over the past 24 hours
I Googled my symptoms and got scared
Work won't let me back without a medical certificate

encounter_type_patient_expectation.description: Optional. The patient's primary expectation from this encounter. Distinct from the clinical need — the AI Doctor should identify and address both. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.ICE_expectations. Useful for scoring whether the AI Doctor addressed hidden agendas.

history_as_reported.description: All historical clinical information as the patient would report it if asked correctly. DELIBERATELY INCOMPLETE relative to the ground_truth_node — patients commonly omit, minimise, misremember, or actively conceal relevant history. What appears here should reflect what the patient would volunteer or confirm in response to a general history-taking inquiry. Specific gated disclosures (things the patient only reveals when asked directly) live in conversational_policy. Maps to Digital Tablet Part A resources.

symptom_narrative.description: The patient's account of their current symptoms in natural language. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags (HPC free-text tags). Each field here represents what the patient would volunteer unprompted or confirm on general questioning — not what requires targeted questioning (which is in conversational_policy). Timeline should be non-linear and vague as real patient recall.

onset.description: When it started — in patient terms, not clinical terms. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.onset. May be imprecise ('a few weeks ago', 'started last Tuesday I think').
examples: been going on for about two weeks
woke up with it this morning

duration.description: How long it has been present. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.duration.
examples: on and off for a few months

character.description: How the patient describes the quality of the symptom. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.character. Use lay descriptors: 'burning', 'tight', 'dull ache', 'sharp'. Avoid clinical terms like 'pleuritic' or 'colicky' unless the patient would use them.

severity_patient_description.description: How bad the patient says it is. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.severity_NRS. Can be NRS 0-10 if patient uses this, or lay description ('pretty bad', 'I can manage', 'worst pain of my life').
examples: pretty uncomfortable but I can manage

location_as_patient_describes.description: Anatomical location in patient language. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.site_radiation and Condition.bodySite. Do NOT use anatomical terminology — 'my chest', 'my lower back', 'the front of my knee', not 'substernal' or 'lumbar'.
examples: my lower back, more on the right side

timing_pattern.description: When it occurs — constant, comes and goes, worse at night, etc. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.timing_pattern.

what_makes_it_better.description: Relieving factors the patient volunteers. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.relieving_factors. Withheld or gated relieving factors (e.g. 'only resting makes it better' when exertional pattern is diagnostically critical) should be placed in conversational_policy instead.
examples: going to the toilet makes it better

what_makes_it_worse.description: Precipitating or aggravating factors the patient volunteers. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.precipitating_factors. As with relieving factors, clinically critical aggravating patterns that require directed questioning belong in conversational_policy.

associated_symptoms_volunteered.description: Associated symptoms the patient offers unprompted. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.associated_symptoms. Only include what the patient would mention without being asked. Gated associated symptoms (e.g. jaw pain, diaphoresis, dyspnoea that the patient only mentions when specifically asked) belong in conversational_policy.
examples: I've also been feeling a bit nauseous
no other symptoms I can think of

previous_episodes.description: History of similar symptoms in the past. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.previous_episodes.
examples: had something similar last year but it went away on its own

functional_impact.description: How it is affecting daily life, work, sleep. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.functional_impact.
examples: had to take a day off work
struggling to look after the kids

past_medical_history.description: Conditions the patient knows about and reports. Maps to Condition resources with clinicalStatus=active/inactive. CRITICAL: this reflects patient self-report, which may omit conditions they consider minor, don't know about, or prefer not to mention. Conditions they would only disclose if asked sensitively belong in conversational_policy. Do NOT include SNOMED codes here — those live in ground_truth_node. Condition names should be as the patient would state them ('diabetes tablets', not 'type 2 diabetes mellitus').

condition_as_patient_states.description: The condition name as the patient would state it — may be imprecise, colloquial, or use trade name thinking ('my blood pressure', 'the diabetes', 'something with my thyroid').
examples: had my appendix out years ago

when_diagnosed.description: Optional. Approximate timing as patient recalls.

how_managed.description: Optional. How the patient reports managing it.

current_medications_as_reported.description: Medications as the patient knows and reports them. Maps to MedicationRequest resources. Patients commonly: use trade names not generics, misremember doses, forget PRN medications, omit OTC or herbal products, and under-report adherence issues. Maps to Digital Tablet MedicationRequest._freetext_medication_tags.adherence_assessment and herbal_OTC_disclosed. Do NOT include AMT SNOMED codes here — those are in ground_truth_node.

name_as_patient_states.description: Medication name as the patient states it — trade name, class description, colour, or partial name all acceptable.

dose_as_patient_reports.description: Optional. Dose as patient recalls — often imprecise.

adherence_as_reported.description: Self-reported adherence. Maps to MedicationRequest._freetext_medication_tags.adherence_assessment. 'recently_stopped' is high-yield — e.g. patient stopped antihypertensives citing side effects.

otc_or_herbal.description: Optional. True if this is an over-the-counter or herbal/complementary medicine. Maps to MedicationRequest._freetext_medication_tags.herbal_OTC_disclosed. OTC/herbal medications are frequently omitted by patients unless specifically asked.

allergies_as_reported.description: Allergies and intolerances as the patient reports them. Maps to AllergyIntolerance resources. AllergyIntolerance.NKA_NKDACodes: include 'no known drug allergy' explicitly when confirmed absent — this has different clinical meaning to not mentioning allergies at all. Patients may over-report side effects as allergies (e.g. 'penicillin allergy' that was actually GI upset). verificationStatus in ground_truth_node may differ.

substance.description: Allergen as patient reports it. Use patient terminology — 'penicillin', 'aspirin', 'peanuts', 'no known allergies'.

reaction_described.description: Optional. How the patient describes the reaction — may reveal whether it is true allergy or side effect.

family_history_as_reported.description: Family history as the patient knows it. Maps to FamilyMemberHistory resources (SNOMED 160303001|FH: condition|). Patients typically know family history imprecisely — 'something with his heart', 'mum had cancer', 'diabetes runs in the family'. Specific genetic testing results or precise diagnoses that require directed questioning belong in conversational_policy. Maps to Digital Tablet FamilyMemberHistory._freetext_family_history_tags.

narrative.description: Family history statement in patient terms. Maps to FamilyMemberHistory._freetext_family_history_tags.positive_FH.
examples: dad had heart trouble in his 60s
breast cancer in the family, not sure which side
no family history of anything serious that I know of

social_history_volunteered.description: Social history as the patient volunteers without prompting. Maps to SDOH_Observations.full_SDOH_field_map. CRITICAL: only include what the patient would mention unprompted. Sensitive SDOH fields (substance use, domestic violence, financial insecurity, mental health history) that require sensitive directed questioning belong in conversational_policy, not here.

smoking_status.description: Smoking history as volunteered. Maps to SDOH Observation SNOMED 229819007 / LOINC 72166-2. SNOMED codes: 8517006|Ex-smoker|, 77176002|Current smoker|, 266919005|Never smoked|.

alcohol.description: Alcohol use as volunteered. Maps to SDOH Observation SNOMED 160573003. Patients typically underestimate. Detailed AUDIT-C screen belongs in conversational_policy.
examples: social only, maybe a glass of wine on weekends

diet_and_exercise.description: Diet and physical activity as described by patient. Maps to SDOH Observations physical_activity_mins_per_wk and diet_quality.
examples: try to walk the dog most days

relevant_life_context.description: Optional. Any life circumstances the patient volunteers that are clinically relevant — work stress, recent bereavement, relationship change, financial stress. Maps to SDOH_Observations and CarePlan barriers_to_care.
examples: been pretty stressed at work lately

psychosocial_profile.description: Optional. Psychosocial context that shapes the consultation dynamic and safety-netting calibration. This block informs how the simulated patient behaves and how the AI Doctor's communication should be scored. Not clinical history per se — it is the frame through which the patient presents their history. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.ICE_ideas / ICE_concerns / ICE_expectations and SDOH_Observations fields.

health_anxiety_level.description: How worried the patient is about their health in general. Affects how symptoms are framed and how safety-netting advice must be pitched. 'low' (stoic, dismissive — needs firm safety-netting); 'catastrophising' (needs reassurance first, then grounding). Maps to SDOH and ClinicalImpression ICE_concerns.

symptom_reporting_style.description: Whether the patient understates, accurately reports, or overstates symptoms. 'minimises': 'it's probably nothing' — their opening complaint undersells severity (common in stoic patients, some males, occupational culture). 'amplifies': describes symptoms in maximally alarming terms regardless of severity. Affects how opening_complaint and history_as_reported should be read.

hidden_agenda.description: Optional. What the patient actually wants from this encounter that they haven't stated outright. The AI Doctor should ideally identify and address this. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.ICE_expectations. Score separately from clinical accuracy.
examples: wants a work certificate to cover today's absence
wants reassurance this isn't cancer after a family member's recent diagnosis
hoping for a specific medication they have read about online
wants something to sleep — hasn't mentioned insomnia yet

communication_style.description: How the patient communicates in the chat interface.

verbosity.description: How much the patient types per message. 'sparse' patients give minimal one-phrase answers — the AI Doctor must work harder to extract information. 'verbose' patients may bury relevant details in lengthy tangential messages.

emotional_tone.description: Predominant emotional tone in messages. Maps to FreeText_Taxonomy MSE affect. Affects how the AI Doctor should respond and the tone scoring.

typical_message_style.description: Optional. Descriptive note on the patient's typing style for the simulated patient engine.
examples: types in short fragments, rarely uses punctuation
writes in complete paragraphs, includes background context
responds with yes/no, needs prompting for detail

reluctance_topics.description: Optional. Topics the patient will NOT raise unprompted and will minimise or deflect if asked indirectly. These MUST have corresponding entries in conversational_policy.disclosure_items with gate 'denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked'. Maps to SDOH high-sensitivity fields: domestic_violence_SAFE, substance use, mental health history, sexual health.

access_barriers.description: Optional. Practical barriers to care that affect what management recommendations are realistic. Maps to CarePlan._freetext_careplan_tags.barriers_to_care and SDOH financial_insecurity, transport_access.

financial.description: Financial barriers affecting medication access, specialist referral, or investigation uptake.

transport.description: Transport limitations. Critical for RA4/RA5 patients where 'go to ED' means significant travel.
examples: no car, relies on family for transport
3 hours from nearest ED by road

other.description: Other access barriers — carer responsibilities, work schedule, disability.
examples: sole carer for three young children
can only attend after 5pm — shift worker
no one to watch the kids if hospitalised

paediatric_context.description: Optional. Populate only when age_band is neonate, infant, child, or adolescent. In paediatric cases the AI Doctor is usually talking to a parent or carer, not the patient. Maps to Digital Tablet FreeText_Taxonomy Paediatric_freetext_tags.

informant.description: Who is providing the history. The AI Doctor must address the informant appropriately and not assume the patient can self-report. 'patient_alone' is only appropriate for adolescents — and may raise safeguarding questions depending on context.

birth_history_relevant.description: Whether birth history is clinically relevant to this case (prematurity, NICU admission, birth complications). Maps to Paediatric_freetext_tags.birth_history. If true, the AI Doctor should ask.

developmental_concerns.description: Optional. Any developmental or school concerns the parent volunteers. Maps to Paediatric_freetext_tags.developmental_milestones.
examples: been a bit behind with talking
teacher has raised concerns about concentration

immunisation_status.description: Optional. Immunisation status as reported by parent. Maps to Immunization.AIR_status and Paediatric_freetext_tags.NIP_immunisation_status.

digital_tablet_field_map.description: Optional. Maps key presentation layer fields to their Digital Tablet fhir_path locations. Used by the case builder and scorer to cross-reference this presentation against the patient record structure. Values are dot-notation paths into digital_tablet_omnibus.json.

three_principles: 1. NO diagnostic spoilers: no SNOMED diagnosis codes, no ICD codes, no ClinicalImpression, no confirmed diagnosis labels, no risk scores. These live in scoring-store nodes only.
2. PATIENT VOICE: all symptom descriptions in natural lay language. No clinical terminology in verbatim_patient_text or symptom_narrative fields.

3. SELECTIVE DISCLOSURE: what appears here is only what the patient volunteers unprompted or confirms on general questioning. What requires directed questioning lives in 02_conversational_policy.json.
_integration_notes.gap_between_presentation_and_ground_trut: The diagnostic challenge of the case is precisely the gap between history_as_reported here and primary_diagnosis in ground_truth_node. A complete, well-volunteered history with no selective disclosures = trivial case. A sparse, minimised, or misleadingly framed history where critical features are gated = difficult case.
what_must_NOT_appear_here: DiagnosticReport results (unless the case specifically involves ordering tests)
Ground truth drug SNOMED codes in AMT format
_integration_notes.conversational_policy_ref: 02_conversational_policy.schema.json (File 10) — selective disclosures that are gated (must be elicited) rather than volunteered are defined there, not here. Every item in reluctance_topics must have a corresponding conversational_policy entry.
_integration_notes.scoring_note: The AI Doctor's score on history-taking quality is measured by how many gated disclosures in conversational_policy it successfully elicits through appropriate questioning. This presentation layer provides the starting information; the conversational policy controls what more the AI Doctor can learn.
